**Bottom line for US LDPE:** North American producers are in the strongest relative position globally — US ethane feedstock provides a $200–400/MT structural cost advantage over naphtha-based competitors, most of whom are either on force majeure (SABIC Jubail, Asian producers) or filing for insolvency (Vynova Europe). European LDPE at a 4-year high of €1,700/MT provides strong export pricing pull. The primary risk for US LDPE buyers is that export demand (Turkey, SE Asia, Europe redirecting away from ME and Asian sources) will pull US supply toward exports, tightening domestic availability and compressing the historical domestic-to-export price spread.
